content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments address real, verified defects. (1) Duration::add panics on overflow — confirmed by compiling and running a test. (2) Process kill only targets the immediate child PID, leaking grandchildren on timeout. (3) Snapshot remains Running during backoff sleep, and resume rejects Running snapshots, creating a stuck-state bug on crash during backoff.

## Votes

- `fr-13-787f9c72`: Accept
  Confirmed by direct test: `Duration::MAX + Duration::from_secs(30)` panics with 'overflow when adding durations'. The Rust std Duration::add calls checked_add().expect(), so it is NOT saturating. Using saturating_add is a trivial, zero-risk fix that eliminates a real panic path. Even though the timeout value is unlikely to be near Duration::MAX in practice, defensive coding at zero cost is the right call.
- `fr-13-43e2123e`: Accept
  Code inspection confirms force_kill() sends SIGKILL to the immediate child PID only. There is no process group creation (setsid/setpgid) at spawn time and no negative-PID or killpg call at kill time. Any backend that forks (shell wrappers, CLI tools that spawn subprocesses) will leave grandchildren running after timeout, leaking resources. This is a real correctness issue for the timeout contract — 'kill on timeout' must mean killing the entire process tree, not just the top-level child.
- `fr-13-997cd9b5`: Accept
  Code inspection confirms all three legs of the bug: (1) snapshot is persisted as RunStatus::Running at line 4472-4481 before agent invocation, (2) after a retryable failure, stage_failed is journaled and backoff sleep occurs (up to 60s) with no snapshot update to a resumable state, (3) resume at line 1155-1159 explicitly rejects Running snapshots. If the process crashes during backoff, the project is permanently stuck — it's Running (so resume rejects it) but no process is active. This is a real data-loss / stuck-state bug. The fix should either persist a resumable state (e.g., Paused or RetryPending) before sleeping, or allow resume to recover stale Running snapshots with appropriate staleness detection.
